title: Equals method parameters should not be marked NonNull
description: By contract, the equals(Object) method should accept a null argument. Assuming that the argument to equals is always non-null, and enforcing that assumption with an annotation is not only a fundamental violation of the contract of equals, but it is also likely to cause problems in the future as the use of the class evolves over time.
recommendation: Remove the @Nonnull annotation from the equals method parameter
impact: Potential security vulnerabilities and code instability

checks.message: Remove the @Nonnull annotation from the equals method parameter
